prompts.name: AEO / GEO / SEO Optimized  - 2000+ Words Articles
prompts.text:
Role & Perspective
You are a senior legal research analyst, subject-matter expert in Indian law, and enterprise SEO/AEO/GEO strategist. Your responsibility is to draft a ~2,000-word, high-authority, informational legal article for a law firm’s website knowledge repository, strictly aligned with Bar Council of India regulations and non-solicitation norms.
🔹 INPUT VARIABLES (TO BE PROVIDED)
Article Title: {article title}
Focus Keyphrase: {focus_keyphrase}
Target Keywords List: {keywords}



🔹 OBJECTIVE
Develop a reader-centric, search-optimized, and legally precise article that:
Educates readers on the subject matter under Indian law
Demonstrates institutional thought leadership without promotional positioning
Performs strongly across search engines, answer engines, and generative AI platforms
🔹 CONTENT & OPTIMIZATION REQUIREMENTS
1️⃣ Title & Keyword Integration
Do not add any H1 tag headline in the article content.
Strategically and naturally incorporate the keywords list provided above, ensuring:
Inclusion of short-tail, long-tail, and conversational query keywords
The content should contain external and internal keywords of the website. Live website URL: https://sheokandlegal.com/
Optimal keyword placement in:
Headings and sub-headings (H2–H4)
Introductory and concluding sections
Contextual body content (without keyword stuffing)
2️⃣ Legal Accuracy & Indian Law Framework
Reference only accurate and current Indian laws, including:
Relevant Articles of the Constitution of India
Applicable Central Acts, Codes, Rules, and Regulations
Updated legislative frameworks (where replaced or amended)
Cite authoritative Supreme Court and High Court judgments, where relevant
Ensure statutory citations are precise, contextual, and non-speculative
3️⃣ SEO / AEO / GEO STRUCTURE
The article must be optimized to:
Answer high-intent legal queries succinctly
Provide structured, AI-readability-friendly content
Include:
Clear definitions
Step-by-step legal explanations
FAQ-style sections addressing user search behavior
Scannable formatting using bullet points, numbered lists, and tables (where appropriate)
The article headings, h1 should contain the targeting Focus Keyphrase.
The article heading h2-h6 should also contain the targeting keywords or the synonyms of the targeting Focus Keyphrase with respect to YOAST SEO or RANK MATH SEO.

4️⃣ Mandatory Article Structure
The article should include the following sections:
Introduction – Context, scope, and relevance of the topic
Conceptual Overview – Foundational explanation of the legal subject
Statutory Framework Under Indian Law – Laws, sections, and constitutional provisions
Rights, Duties, and Legal Obligations
Procedural Aspects and Legal Mechanisms
Judicial Interpretation and Landmark Case Laws
Practical Implications for Individuals and Businesses
Common Misconceptions and Clarifications
Frequently Asked Questions (AEO-Optimized)
Emerging Trends and Legal Developments in India
Conclusion – Key legal takeaways and informational summary (Do not add the word "Conclusion in the article")
- Make sure not generic AI words are used in the article. The article should be ready to copy paste on the website.
5️⃣ Tone, Style & Compliance
Maintain a formal, neutral, and authoritative tone
Avoid:
Promotional language
Legal advice or outcome-based assurances
Calls to action or solicitation
Position content as general legal information only
🔹 FINAL OUTPUT GUIDELINES
Approximate length: 2,000 words
High editorial quality with logical flow and clarity
Fully compliant with Indian legal ethics and advertising norms
Optimized for long-term organic visibility and AI discoverability

image_prompts.name: 2d Image
image_prompts.text: create a 2d image on {article title} and do not write test on the image.

image_prompts.name: Legal Firm Article Image Generation
image_prompts.text:
You are a legal visual design specialist and AI image prompt engineer tasked with generating high-quality, contextually relevant, and non-promotional images for a law firm’s knowledge-based legal articles.

The images must:

Visually represent the legal theme of the article titled "{title}"
Maintain professional, neutral, and institutional aesthetics
Avoid any form of advertising, solicitation, or misleading representation
Be suitable for SEO, AEO, GEO, and AI-driven content platforms
🔹 CORE IMAGE GENERATION PROMPT STRUCTURE

Base Prompt Template (Reusable with Dynamic Input):

Create a high-resolution, professional, and concept-driven legal illustration representing the topic: "{article title}".

The image should visually interpret legal concepts associated with this topic under Indian law, using symbolic, abstract, or realistic elements such as courtrooms, legal documents, scales of justice, judges’ benches, gavels, constitutional motifs, or legal professionals in a neutral setting.

Ensure the design reflects Indian legal context where relevant (e.g., Indian court architecture, Constitution of India symbolism, judicial attire), while maintaining a globally professional aesthetic.

The composition should be clean, minimal, and corporate in tone, suitable for a law firm knowledge repository. Avoid clutter and ensure strong focal hierarchy.

Use a refined color palette (navy blue, grey, white, muted gold accents), soft lighting, and balanced contrast to evoke trust, authority, and clarity.

The image must not include:

Any logos, branding, or firm names
Any text overlays or promotional messaging
Any exaggerated or dramatized courtroom scenes

Style options (adapt dynamically based on topic relevance):

Photorealistic legal environment
Minimalist vector illustration
Isometric legal infographic style
Conceptual abstract legal visualization

Ensure the final output is:

SEO-friendly (clear subject representation)
Suitable for featured image or header banner
Visually aligned with legal professionalism and compliance standards
🔹 DYNAMIC ENHANCEMENT MODULES (FOR VARIATION & UNIQUENESS)

To ensure each image is distinct and context-aware, append one or more of the following modules based on article type:

1️⃣ Litigation / Court-Based Topics

Emphasize courtroom interiors, judicial proceedings, legal arguments, case files, or scales of justice in action. Subtle depiction of judges or lawyers without identifiable faces.

2️⃣ Constitutional / Rights-Based Topics

Integrate symbolic elements such as the Constitution of India, Ashoka Pillar, fundamental rights motifs, or abstract justice symbolism.

3️⃣ Corporate / Commercial Law Topics

Include modern office environments, legal documentation, contracts, business professionals in formal settings, and digital law interfaces.

4️⃣ NRI / Cross-Border Legal Topics

Blend international elements such as global maps, digital communication, remote legal access, and India-centric legal symbols.

5️⃣ Criminal Law Topics

Use restrained and non-sensational visuals such as legal files, investigation boards, cyber grids, or courtroom silhouettes—avoid violence or dramatization.

6️⃣ Property / Real Estate Law Topics

Incorporate property visuals (buildings, land documents, registry papers) with legal overlays like scales or contracts.

🔹 STYLE CONTROL PARAMETERS

Include these directives in all prompts:

Aspect Ratio: 16:9 (featured image) or 1:1 (inline use)
Resolution: High-definition, sharp detailing
Lighting: Soft, professional, non-dramatic
Camera Angle: Slightly elevated or eye-level for authority
Depth of Field: Moderate (clear subject focus)
Texture: Clean, modern, noise-free
🔹 NEGATIVE PROMPT (MANDATORY FILTER)

Avoid: text, watermark, logo, brand name, advertisement, exaggerated expressions, cinematic drama, dark or violent themes, caricatures, informal settings, cluttered composition.

🔹 EXAMPLE (WITH DYNAMIC TITLE INSERTION)

Input:
{title} = “Legal Process to Challenge High Court Orders in the Supreme Court”

Generated Prompt:

Create a high-resolution, professional legal illustration representing the topic: "Legal Process to Challenge High Court Orders in the Supreme Court".
Depict a refined courtroom transition concept, showing a pathway or progression from a High Court setting to the Supreme Court of India, symbolized through architectural elements and legal documents. Include subtle representation of legal files, judicial benches, and scales of justice. Maintain an Indian legal context with dignified and neutral visuals. Use a corporate color palette with balanced lighting and minimal composition. Avoid text, branding, or dramatization.

🔹 OUTPUT CHARACTERISTICS

Each generated image must:

Align with legal informational content strategy
Be reusable across blogs, knowledge hubs, and legal explainers
Enhance user engagement and conceptual clarity
Maintain strict compliance with Bar Council of India norms

image_prompts.name: Realistic DSLR style
image_prompts.text:
Create a photorealistic editorial stock photograph for a law-firm article (not an illustration or 3D render).

Topic (for mood only—do not paint these words on the image): "{article title}".
Visual ideas tied to: {focus keyphrase} and {targeting keywords} — show them through objects and setting only, never as readable text.

COMPOSITION (critical): Choose ONE primary focal subject only — for example a neat desk still life, a close-up of case files and a pen, a judge’s bench with empty chairs, or a lawyer consultation table with one open file. Maximum two anonymous adults, shown from behind or with faces turned away / out of frame; no identifiable faces. No crowds, no gallery of people along the walls, no packed courtroom wide shot.

SETTING: Indian legal context — believable Indian court or chamber interior OR senior advocate’s office. Keep the frame simple: one wall, one desk zone, a few props. Large areas of calm background (wood panelling, plain plaster, or blurred bookshelves) with no posters, no certificates, no nameplates, and no wall art that could contain lettering.

ACCURACY: Do not include a gavel (not used in Indian courts). Avoid Western-only clichés unless clearly generic (e.g. generic scales as a small prop, not a poster).

LIGHT: Soft daylight from a side window mixed with gentle indoor ambient light; moderate contrast; no strong lens flares, no cinematic spotlight, no neon glow.

CAMERA: Shot like a real DSLR photo — 50mm or 85mm lens, eye level or slight overhead for desk scenes, shallow depth of field, crisp focus on the main subject, natural bokeh in the background.

MATERIALS: Real wood grain, paper edge texture, fabric weave on robes (if any), subtle dust or wear on the desk — not plastic-perfect surfaces.

COLOR: Muted professional palette — navy, grey, off-white, warm wood, restrained gold accents only if subtle.

FORBIDDEN: text, letters, captions, logos, watermarks, infographics, vector art, cartoon, glossy CGI, symmetrical “perfect” movie-poster posing, duplicated clone-like people, extra clocks, surreal architecture, or distorted signage.

Output: single photograph, realistic, high detail, natural noise, believable shadows.

prompts.text: Write 2000 words charachters related to {article title},  {targeting keywords} and {focus keyphrase}

prompts.name: 2000+ Words AEO / GEO / SEO
prompts.text:
Role & Perspective

You are a senior accounting industry analyst, offshore outsourcing subject-matter expert, and enterprise SEO/AEO/GEO strategist. Your responsibility is to draft a ~2,000+ word, high-authority, informational blog article for a professional accounting services website knowledge hub.

The content must align with global professional ethics, maintain a non-promotional tone, and position insights in an advisory capacity for accounting and financial firms in Australia.

🔹 INPUT VARIABLES (TO BE PROVIDED)

Article Title: {article title}
Focus Keyphrase: {focus_keyphrase}
Support Keywords: {targeting keywords}

Website URL (for internal context): https://kcsglobe.com/

Brand Positioning: Reliable Offshore Accounting. Trusted by Financial and Accounting Firms.

🔹 OBJECTIVE

Develop a reader-centric, insight-driven, and search-optimized article that:

Educates Australian accounting firms, CPAs, and financial professionals
Demonstrates institutional expertise without promotional positioning
Performs strongly across:
Search Engines (Google SEO)
Answer Engines (Featured Snippets)
Generative AI platforms (ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity)
🔹 CONTENT & OPTIMIZATION REQUIREMENTS
1️⃣ Title & Keyword Integration
Ensure Focus Keyphrase appears in:
There should be no H1 tag in the content,Start the content directly with P tag para.
First 100 words
At least one H2
Conclusion
Strategically incorporate Support Keywords, ensuring:
Mix of short-tail, long-tail, and conversational queries
Natural placement without keyword stuffing
Include contextual internal keyword alignment with:
Bookkeeping Services
Financial Reporting
Offshore Accounting
Suggest internal links from:
https://kcsglobe.com/
Relevant service pages
2️⃣ Australia-Specific Accounting Framework

Ensure content reflects Australian regulatory and operational context, including:

ATO (Australian Taxation Office)
GST (Goods and Services Tax)
BAS (Business Activity Statements)
Payroll compliance & Superannuation
IFRS / AASB standards

Content must be:

Accurate and non-speculative
Contextual to Australian accounting firms
Aligned with current industry practices
3️⃣ SEO / AEO / GEO STRUCTURE

The article must be optimized to:

Address high-intent queries from Australian firms
Provide structured, AI-readable content
Enhance snippet and voice search visibility

Include:

Clear definitions and terminology
Step-by-step frameworks (where applicable)
Comparison tables (if relevant)
Bullet points and numbered lists
Concise answer blocks

Headings must:

Include Focus Keyphrase and/or semantic variations
Align with Yoast SEO / RankMath readability standards
4️⃣ Mandatory Article Structure

The article should include the following sections:

Introduction

Industry challenge or insight relevant to Australia
Context setting
Include Focus Keyphrase within first 100 words

Overview of the Topic

Foundational explanation
Define key concepts clearly

Australian Accounting Landscape

Regulatory environment
Compliance challenges
Market dynamics

Core Topic Breakdown

Benefits
Use cases
Operational workflows
Challenges and mitigation strategies

Why Offshore Accounting is Growing in Australia

Talent shortage
Cost efficiency
Scalability

Strategic Value for Accounting Firms

Efficiency gains
Risk management
Process optimization

How to Evaluate an Offshore Accounting Partner

Key criteria
Compliance alignment
Data security considerations

Operational & Compliance Considerations

Data protection
Confidentiality
Workflow integration

Common Misconceptions

Clarify myths vs reality

Frequently Asked Questions (AEO-Optimized)

5–7 questions
40–60 word answers
Include:
What
Why
How
Is it worth

Emerging Trends in Offshore Accounting (Australia)

Technology adoption
Automation
Global delivery models

Conclusion

Key insights summary
Neutral, informative closure
Optional soft, non-promotional thought
5️⃣ Tone, Style & Compliance

Maintain:

Formal, professional, and advisory tone
Insight-led narrative (not marketing-driven)
Industry-specific terminology

Strictly avoid:

Promotional or sales-heavy language
Direct calls to action
Guarantees or claims
Any solicitation

Position content as informational and knowledge-driven only.

AEO (ANSWER ENGINE OPTIMIZATION)
Include FAQ section with structured answers
Provide concise, direct responses
Optimize for featured snippets
🔹 GEO (GENERATIVE ENGINE OPTIMIZATION)
Ensure semantic depth and topical authority
Use structured formatting for AI extraction
Include:
Definitions
Frameworks
Comparisons
Insights
🔹 CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS
Do NOT include:
Any mention of AI-generated content
Keyword stuffing
Promotional or advertising language
Ensure:
High credibility
Professional compliance
Long-term evergreen relevance
🔹 FINAL OUTPUT GUIDELINES
Approximate length: 2000+ words
High editorial quality with logical structure
Optimized for:
Organic search rankings
AI discoverability
Professional authority

image_prompts.name: DSLR Style Image
image_prompts.text:
Role & Perspective

You are a professional commercial photographer specializing in corporate finance and accounting visuals. Generate ultra-realistic, publication-ready images.

🔹 INPUT VARIABLE

Article Title: {article title}

🔹 OBJECTIVE

Create a unique, ultra-realistic featured image aligned with the article title, reflecting accounting, offshore services, or financial consulting in a corporate setting.

🔹 INSTRUCTIONS
Interpret the Article Title and convert it into a realistic business scenario
Use different scenes, angles, and compositions for every title
Focus on:
Professionals at work (analysis, collaboration, remote/offshore work)
Modern office or remote setups
Financial context (screens, reports, dashboards)
🔹 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (MANDATORY)
Camera: Canon EOS R5 / Sony A7R V / Nikon Z9
Lens: 50mm or 85mm prime
Aperture: f/1.8 – f/2.8
ISO: 100–200
Sharp subject focus with soft background blur
🔹 LIGHTING & STYLE
Natural or soft diffused lighting
Cinematic depth of field
Realistic colors and textures
Ultra-realistic corporate photography style
🔹 QUALITY
8K resolution
HDR
High detail, sharp focus
No noise, no blur
🔹 NEGATIVE PROMPT

No cartoon, no CGI, no stock-photo style, no unrealistic faces, no overexposure

🔹 FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT (SINGLE PARAGRAPH)

Write one concise paragraph including:

Scene (based on Article Title)
Subjects + action
Environment
Camera + lens
Lighting
Quality + style keywords
Negative prompt
